text: Oral iron supplementation significantly increases serum ferritin concentration in athletes compared to control groups.
Oral iron supplementation does not significantly increase blood haemoglobin levels in athletes.
Oral iron supplementation shows a trend toward small positive effect on VO2max in young healthy athletes, but the effect does not reach statistical significance.
Oral iron supplementation is most effective at raising serum ferritin in athletes whose pre-supplementation ferritin is at or below 12 µg/l, with minimal effect at higher baseline levels.
Effective oral iron supplementation doses for improving hematological parameters in athletes range from 16 to 100 mg of elementary iron daily.
Oral iron supplementation protocols shorter than 6 weeks are ineffective for improving blood iron status in athletes; beneficial effects require 6 to 8 weeks of supplementation.
Oral iron supplementation does not significantly alter transferrin saturation or serum transferrin receptor concentration in athletes.
